I understand you want me to warm up. Let me explore the codebase to get familiar with the project structure.

<glob>
pattern: **/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx,vue,py,java,go,rs,cpp,h} | head -20
</glob>

<ls>
path: /home/aryaniyaps/web-projects/moodle-proctor
</ls>

<git log --oneline -10>
</git log>

<read>
path: /home/aryaniyaps/web-projects/moodle-proctor/README.md
</read>

<read>
path: /home/aryaniyaps/web-projects/moodle-proctor/package.json
</read>

The project appears to be a Moodle proctoring system with AI capabilities. From the git status, I can see we're on the `feature/multi-room-proctoring` branch with modifications to Docker files and docker-compose. The recent commits show work on multi-room components, room creation modal, and SSE integration with room-based filtering.

Key files and directories observed:
- AI proctoring component (`ai_proctoring/`)
- Backend service (`backend/`)
- Docker configuration files
- TypeScript/JavaScript components (based on file extensions)
- Recent work on multi-room proctoring features

The system appears to be using Docker for containerization and has been actively developed with focus on room-based proctoring functionality.
